pages.raw_text:
BOISE, Idaho, Feb. 21.-Moyer,
Haywood and Pettibone, in charge
of deputy warden and guards,
were taken to Caldwell this morn
ing for arraignment. The train
stopped opposite the county Jail
and the prisoners were transferred.
At 10 o'clock they were brought
before Probate Judge Church,
where an information was read
charging each with numbering ex-
Governor Steunenberg. The pris-
oners were represented by E. F.
Richardson of Denver. The latter
had no legal authority to appear,
not having been admitted to prac-
tice in Idaho. The courtesy was
extended at the request of Prose-
cutting Attorney Van Duyn.


Pettibone was first arraigned and
an affidavit read charging him with
exploding the bomb by which
Frank Steunenberg met his deat tn
